{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":489200,"duration_api_ms":489119,"ttft_ms":6751,"num_turns":42,"result":"CP00-180 review complete: **PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (3 P3 advisories, 0 P0/P1; does not block pack or goal closeout).

Verdict basis, confirmed against live source:
- **No over-claim** — `production_ready=false` is consistent across manifest, construction-inspection (PENDING), adjudication ("no"), and command-evidence (in_progress).
- **No-write/no-real-data boundary holds** — `service.js:10` `freezeServiceResult` stamps all write/permission/audit/lock/network/loop flags `false`; descriptor-only, fail-closed to customer-safe error codes.
- **Authority boundary preserved** — contract + registry set `claude_is_final_approval`/`promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false` and no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation; run is read-only.
- **CP178+ hardening intact** — readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON), so not an auth/0-byte/malformed-JSON attempt; `forbidden_review_evidence` codified and reused.
- **Hermes confined** to deterministic packet/evidence generation, not product runtime.

The three P3s are non-blocking: downstream pipeline bookkeeping still pending (R01, R02) and a functionally-harmless validator carryover from CP00-179 (R03). Implementation matches contract/baseline: deliverable distribution implementation:6/security_audit:2/ui:2 and RP05.P02.M09=10 are enforced by validateMatterCoreCp180Coverage; the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds (service.js freezeServiceResult stamps writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, creates/updates/deletes_database_rows, acquires_runtime_lock, dispatches_review/approval_route, executes_api_handler, issues_network_request, implements_loop_engine = false; lock_status "not_acquired_descriptor_only"; persistence_boundary "never_write_descriptor_only"); sensitive prechecks fail closed to customer-safe error codes (e.g. tenant_boundary_mismatch->MATTER_CORE_TENANT_BOUNDARY_MISMATCH, missing_matter_trace->MATTER_CORE_MATTER_TRACE_REQUIRED, idempotency_key_required->MATTER_CORE_IDEMPOTENCY_REQUIRED). Authority boundary is preserved at every layer: contract.sensitive_service_precheck_boundary and contract.no_write_attestation plus registry MATTER_CORE_CP180_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION set claude_is_final_approval / promotes_claude_to_final_approval = false and enterprise_trust_claim_from_local_validation / claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation = false; validateMatterCoreCp180SensitiveBoundary asserts these, and the run is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob). CP178+ review hardening is intact: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit_code 0, 1434-byte valid-JSON stdout, review_attempt_counted=false), so this is not an auth-failure / 0-byte / malformed-JSON / fenced-JSON / tool-call-shaped attempt, and forbidden_review_evidence (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, ...) is codified in registry/contract and reused by the CP180 Claude packet. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic control-plane packet/evidence generation (createMatterCoreCp180HermesEvidencePacket; H05 command-evidence with spec_requirement_validate + rp00_control_plane_contract_validate), not product runtime. No P0/P1 defects found. Three P3 advisories cover expected mid-pipeline bookkeeping (downstream construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence pending with empty command sets; empty claude-review-result ledger) and one functionally-harmless carryover (generic review_status-vs-lifecycle coupling in validators.js, also noted in the CP00-179 review). The local/Hermes validation command results (the 11 validation_commands) are not yet captured into pack artifacts. This is the expected state for a pack positioned before its pack-level Claude review is normalized, and it is honestly reflected (manifest.production_ready=false) rather than over-claimed, so it is advisory rather than a defect.","recommendation":"Before commit, complete the hardened pipeline (normalize -> receipt_validate -> adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation) so command-evidence.commands, construction-inspection.tests_and_validation.commands, and the H05/C05 gate booleans are populated from real local runs, and only then resolve production_ready.","post_review_disposition":"advisory_open — non-blocking; expected mid-pipeline state to be completed by downstream hardened-review steps before commit"},{"id":"C-CP180-R02","severity":"P3","title":"claude-review-result ledger arrays are empty and exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run is false","file":"docs/closeout-packs/cp00-180/claude-review-result.json","line":19,"details":"valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] with exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false and status=review_pending; no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json exists yet for CP00-180. This is accurate for a review that has not yet been normalized into a receipt: the readiness auth_preflight passed and review_attempt_counted=false, so there is also no invalid attempt to log. Non-blocking bookkeeping.","recommendation":"After this read-only review is captured and validated by the hardened pipeline, append the resulting valid receipt to valid_review_receipts, set exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=true, and confirm review-receipt.json/raw-output.json refs resolve before closeout.","post_review_disposition":"advisory_open — non-blocking; bookkeeping to be populated when this review receipt is normalized"},{"id":"C-CP180-R03","severity":"P3","title":"validateMatterCoreRecord validates review_status against lifecycle_statuses for all model types","file":"packages/matter/src/validators.js","line":74,"details":"record.review_status is validated against definition.lifecycle_statuses generically for every model type. Only MatterWikiSection (whose lifecycle_statuses are the review vocabulary) and the graph node/edge models carry a meaningful review_status; for other models review_status is absent, so the guarded check (record?.review_status && ...) is a harmless no-op. The behavior is functionally correct and fail-closed, but it conflates lifecycle and review-status vocabularies. This is a carryover of the CP00-179 review observation (CP179-R03) and validators.js is an in-scope CP00-180 implementation ref.","recommendation":"Optionally add a per-model review_status_vocabulary (or scope the review_status check to models that declare one) so lifecycle state and review state are not validated against the same list.
